---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)

source("R/utils.R")
```

```{r}
# path to data folder on sharepoint
sp_data_to_plot_path <- csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data")

state_name <- "United States"
state_name_text <- "the United States"

# read in crime data
srs_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_us.rds")) |> 
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)
srs_by_cat_us <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_by_cat_us.rds")) |> 
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2012)

### import state-level file for mapping
### aggregate counts and create rates
### only need 2019 and 2022 (to calculate change between the two)
### QUESTION: should i use this file (which seems to use estimates or the agency-level file)
### do we need a pop covered filter for 2022?
### TO DO: use agency-level file and remove NYPD -- their reporting of incidents and not clearances
### appears to be shifting ny unsolved rate from ~40% in 2019 to ~90% in 2022
### perhaps florida, as well 
srs_state_to_map <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds")) |> 
  filter(crime_cat == "Violent", 
         year%in%c(2019,2022)) |> 
   dplyr::group_by(state_name,
                   state_abbr,
                   pop_total,
                   year) |> 
  summarize(n_reported = sum(incidents_reported,
                             na.rm = TRUE),
         n_cleared = sum(incidents_cleared,
                             na.rm = TRUE),
         n_unsolved = sum(incidents_unsolved,
                             na.rm = TRUE)) |> 
  ### calculate rates
  mutate(unsolved_rate = n_unsolved/n_reported,
         viol_crime_rate_100k = (n_reported/pop_total)*100000) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  group_by(state_name) |> 
  ### calculate change over time
  mutate(unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = (unsolved_rate[year==2019] - unsolved_rate[year==2022])/unsolved_rate[year==2019],
         viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = (viol_crime_rate_100k[year==2019] - viol_crime_rate_100k[year==2022])/viol_crime_rate_100k[year==2019]) |> 
    ungroup() |> 
  ### now drop 2019
  ### and keep counts/rates from 2022
  filter(year==2022)  |> 
  ### currently multiplying rate by 100 instead of using scales::percent 
  ### may be better way to format within hc
  mutate(unsolved_rate = unsolved_rate*100,
         unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022*100,
         viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022 = viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022*100)

min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_us |> 
  filter(year %in% c(min(year), max(year))) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1] 

pct_change <- (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported[1]

change_text <- case_when(
  pct_change > 0.005  ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                                    " more"),
  pct_change < -0.005 ~ paste0(percent(abs(pct_change), 1),
                                    " fewer"),
  TRUE                ~ "nearly the same number of"
  )

pct_change_unsolved <- min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[1]

change_text_unsolved <- case_when(
  pct_change_unsolved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point worse than"),
  pct_change_unsolved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points worse than"),
  pct_change_unsolved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point better than"),
  pct_change_unsolved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points better than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )

max_viol_crime_off <- srs_us |> 
  filter(year == max(year)) |> 
  arrange(desc(incidents_reported)) |> 
  mutate(pct = incidents_reported / sum(incidents_reported))
```


```{r}
# set up hex map functions

map_colors <- list(
  list(name = "Increase",     from = 1, to = 1, color = jr_pal[2]),
  list(name = "Decrease",     from = 2, to = 2, color = jr_pal[3]),
  list(name = "No 2022 data", from = 3, to = 3, color = "darkgray")
)

map_colors_rev <- list(
  list(name = "Increase",     from = 1, to = 1, color = jr_pal[1]),
  list(name = "Decrease",     from = 2, to = 2, color = jr_pal[4]),
  list(name = "No 2022 data", from = 3, to = 3, color = "darkgray")
)

make_hex_map <- function(df, colors) {
  highchart()  |>
    hc_add_series_map(
      map = hex_gj,
      df = df,
      joinBy = "state_abb",
      value = "status",
      dataLabels = list(enabled = TRUE, format = "{point.state_abb}",
                        style = list(fontSize = "13px",
                                     fontFamily = "GT America",
                                     fontWeight = 700,
                                     textOutline = 0))
    )  |>
    hc_colorAxis(
      dataClassColor = "category",
      dataClasses = colors
    ) |>
    hc_legend(
      itemStyle = list(fontSize = "14px",
                       fontFamily = "GT America",
                       fontWeight = 400)
    ) |>
    hc_tooltip(enabled = FALSE) |>
    hc_plotOptions(
      series = list(
        animation = FALSE,
        states = list(inactive = list(opacity = 1)),
        cursor = "pointer",
        accessibility = list(
          enabled = TRUE,
          keyboardNavigation = list(enabled = TRUE)
        ),
        point = list(
          events = list(
            click = JS("function(){parent.document.location.href = this.options.url}"))
        )
      )
    ) |>
    hc_add_dependency(name = "modules/accessibility.js")
}

hex <- sf::read_sf("us_states_hexgrid.geojson") |>
  select(state_abb = iso3166_2)

hex_gj <- hex |>
  sf::st_transform(3857) |>
  geojsonsf::sf_geojson() |>
  jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE)

```

```{r}
### prep df for hex map

# viol_crime_change <- viol_crime_2021_state |>
#   # filter(pop_cov >= 0.9) |>
#   transmute(
#     state,
#     viol_crime_21 = actual_est,
#     viol_crime_rate_21 = actual_est / pop_total,
#     viol_crime_clear_21 = clearance_rate
#     ) |>
#   full_join(filter(viol_crime_by_state, year == 2019), by = "state") |>
#   transmute(state, state_abb, viol_crime_21, viol_crime_rate_21, viol_crime_clear_21,
#             viol_crime_19 = actual_est, viol_crime_rate_19 = actual_est / pop_total,
#             viol_crime_clear_19 = clearance_rate)


to_map_viol_crime <- srs_state_to_map |>
  dplyr::select(state=state_name,
                state_abb=state_abbr,
                pct_change=viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022) |> 
  mutate(status = case_when(
      pct_change > 0 ~ 1,
      pct_change <= 0 ~ 2,
      is.na(pct_change) ~ 3,
    ),
    url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abb)))

# to_map_viol_crime_clear <- viol_crime_change |>
#   mutate(
#     change = viol_crime_clear_21 - viol_crime_clear_19,
#     pct_change = change / viol_crime_clear_19,
#     status = case_when(
#       pct_change > 0 ~ 1,
#       pct_change <= 0 ~ 2,
#       is.na(pct_change) ~ 3,
#     ),
#     url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abb))
#   )
# 
# hom_change <- viol_crime_2021_by_off_state |>
#   filter(pop_cov >= 0.9, crime == "Homicide") |>
#   transmute(
#     state,
#     hom_21 = actual,
#     hom_rate_21 = actual / pop_total,
#     hom_clear_21 = clearance_rate
#     ) |>
#   full_join(filter(viol_crime_by_off_state, year == 2019, crime == "Homicide"), by = "state") |>
#   transmute(state, state_abb, hom_21, hom_rate_21, hom_clear_21,
#             hom_19 = actual, hom_rate_19 = actual / pop_total, hom_clear_19 = clearance_rate)
# 
# to_map_hom <- hom_change |>
#   mutate(
#     change = hom_rate_21 - hom_rate_19,
#     pct_change = change / hom_rate_19,
#     status = case_when(
#       pct_change > 0 ~ 1,
#       pct_change <= 0 ~ 2,
#       is.na(pct_change) ~ 3,
#     ),
#     url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abb))
#   )
# 
# to_map_hom_clear <- hom_change |>
#   mutate(
#     change = hom_clear_21 - hom_clear_19,
#     pct_change = change / hom_clear_19,
#     status = case_when(
#       pct_change > 0 ~ 1,
#       pct_change <= 0 ~ 2,
#       is.na(pct_change) ~ 3,
#     ),
#     url = paste0("https://projects.csgjusticecenter.org/tools-for-states-to-address-crime/50-state-crime-data/?state=", tolower(state_abb))
#   )

make_hex_map(to_map_viol_crime, map_colors)

# hom_hex <- make_hex_map(to_map_hom, map_colors)

# viol_crime_clear_hex <- make_hex_map(to_map_viol_crime_clear, map_colors_rev)
# hom_clear_hex <- make_hex_map(to_map_hom_clear, map_colors_rev)
# 
# 
# saveWidget(viol_crime_hex, "viol-crime-hex-map.html", selfcontained = FALSE, libdir = "map-libs")
# saveWidget(hom_hex, "hom-hex-map.html", selfcontained = FALSE, libdir = "map-libs")
# 
# saveWidget(viol_crime_clear_hex, "viol-crime-clear-hex-map.html", selfcontained = FALSE, libdir = "map-libs")
# saveWidget(hom_clear_hex, "hom-clear-hex-map.html", selfcontained = FALSE, libdir = "map-libs")
```



# United States data on violent crime and arrests


## Mapping violent crime rates


::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2022 Violent crime rate 

```{r}
### to do: add legend title, additional info in tooltip, accessibility? 
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-all"),
  data = srs_state_to_map,
  value = "viol_crime_rate_100k",
  joinBy = c("hc-a2","state_abbr"),
  name = "2022 Violent Crime Rate",
  borderColor = "#FAFAFA",
  borderWidth = 0.1,
  tooltip = list(valueDecimals = 1,
    valueSuffix = " incidents reported per 100k")) 

```


## Change in violent crime rate 2019 to 2022 by state

```{r}
### to do: add legend label, additional data in tooltip, colors?


### add diverging scale to hc map
### reference: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/54143215/setting-heat-map-color-range-as-per-given-sequence-in-hcharter

p <- (0 - min(srs_state_to_map$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022)) / (max(srs_state_to_map$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022) - min(srs_state_to_map$viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022))

stops <- data.frame(q = c(0, p, 1),
                    c = c("#00475d", "#FFFFFF","#f89c1b"),
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


stops <- list_parse2(stops)


### to do: add legend title, additional info in tooltip, accessibility? 
### fix diverging scale (set 0 as middle point)
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-all"),
  data = srs_state_to_map,
  value = "viol_crime_rate_percent_change_2019_2022",
  joinBy = c("hc-a2","state_abbr"),
  name = "Percent Change in Violent Crime Rate, 2019 to 2022",
  borderColor = "#000000",
  borderWidth = 0.15,
  tooltip = list(valueDecimals = 1,
    valueSuffix = "% change")) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(stops = stops)


```

:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Background on crime data reporting (edit)

Beginning with data year 2021, the FBI discontinued collecting Uniform Crime Reporting data via the Summary Reporting System and required law enforcement agencies to use the [National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS)](https://bjs.ojp.gov/national-incident-based-reporting-system-nibrs){target="_parent"} to report crime statistics to the FBI. Because many states did not have full NIBRS reporting coverage in 2021, the FBI and Bureau of Justice Statistics [generated estimated crime statistics](https://bjs.ojp.gov/library/publications/estimation-procedures-crimes-united-states-based-nibrs-data){target="_parent"} based on the agencies that reported data to the FBI. The reported crime and clearance rates for 2021 have a level of uncertainty represented by a margin of error for each estimate because not all agencies reported data to the FBI.

:::

## Mapping unsolved violent crime rates


::: {.panel-tabset}

## 2022 Violent crime unsolved rate 

```{r}
### remove illinois
srs_state_to_map_unsolved <- srs_state_to_map |> 
  filter(state_abbr!="IL")

### to do: add legend title, additional info in tooltip, accessibility? 
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-all"),
  data = srs_state_to_map_unsolved,
  value = "unsolved_rate",
  joinBy = c("hc-a2","state_abbr"),
  name = "2022 Unsolved Violent Crime Rate",
  borderColor = "#FAFAFA",
  borderWidth = 0.1,
  tooltip = list(valueDecimals = 1,
    valueSuffix = "%")) 

```


## Change in violent crime unsolved rate 2019 to 2022

**to do:** remove nypd? other outliers in florida?

```{r}
### to do: add legend label, additional data in tooltip, colors?


### add diverging scale to hc map
### reference: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/54143215/setting-heat-map-color-range-as-per-given-sequence-in-hcharter

### first, find where the position where zero is located
position_zero <- (0 - min(srs_state_to_map_unsolved$unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022)) / (max(srs_state_to_map_unsolved$unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022) - min(srs_state_to_map_unsolved$unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022))

### then create a list where we set min/max colors and white as our color for zero
stops <- data.frame(q = c(0, position_zero, 1),
                    c = c("#00475d", "#FFFFFF","#f89c1b"),
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


stops <- list_parse2(stops)


### to do: add legend title, additional info in tooltip, accessibility? 
### fix diverging scale 
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-all"),
  data = srs_state_to_map_unsolved,
  value = "unsolved_rate_percent_change_2019_2022",
  joinBy = c("hc-a2","state_abbr"),
  name = "Percent Change in Unsolved Violent Crime Rate, 2019 to 2022",
  borderColor = "#000000",
  borderWidth = 0.15,
  tooltip = list(valueDecimals = 1,
    valueSuffix = "% change")) |> 
  hc_colorAxis(stops = stops)
```

:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Background on crime data reporting (edit)

Beginning with data year 2021, the FBI discontinued collecting Uniform Crime Reporting data via the Summary Reporting System and required law enforcement agencies to use the [National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS)](https://bjs.ojp.gov/national-incident-based-reporting-system-nibrs){target="_parent"} to report crime statistics to the FBI. Because many states did not have full NIBRS reporting coverage in 2021, the FBI and Bureau of Justice Statistics [generated estimated crime statistics](https://bjs.ojp.gov/library/publications/estimation-procedures-crimes-united-states-based-nibrs-data){target="_parent"} based on the agencies that reported data to the FBI. The reported crime and clearance rates for 2021 have a level of uncertainty represented by a margin of error for each estimate because not all agencies reported data to the FBI.

:::



## Violent crime in the United States

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
srs_by_cat_us |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    ) |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |>
  hc_setup()
```


```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year) |> 
  mutate(tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported)) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide")

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(min = 0) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_2$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_3$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |> 
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

to_plot_5 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_5$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |> 
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

to_plot_4 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot_4$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em><br>The FBI <a href='https://ucr.fbi.gov/recent-program-updates/new-rape-definition-frequently-asked-questions'  target='_parent'>broadened the definition of rape<a/> for crime reporting in 2013") |>
  hc_legend(enabled = FALSE) |> 
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |> 
  hc_setup()
```

:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Background on crime data reporting

Beginning with data year 2021, the FBI discontinued collecting Uniform Crime Reporting data via the Summary Reporting System and required law enforcement agencies to use the [National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS)](https://bjs.ojp.gov/national-incident-based-reporting-system-nibrs){target="_parent"} to report crime statistics to the FBI. Because many states did not have full NIBRS reporting coverage in 2021, the FBI and Bureau of Justice Statistics [generated estimated crime statistics](https://bjs.ojp.gov/library/publications/estimation-procedures-crimes-united-states-based-nibrs-data){target="_parent"} based on the agencies that reported data to the FBI. The reported crime and clearance rates for 2021 have a level of uncertainty represented by a margin of error for each estimate because not all agencies reported data to the FBI.

:::

## Unsolved violent crime in `r state_name_text`

```{r}
unsolved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")
```

In `r max_year`, `r unsolved_rate_max` of violent crimes reported to police were not solved. This is `r change_text_unsolved` the unsolved rate in `r min_year`.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
srs_by_cat_us |> 
  mutate(
    pct_unsolved = pct_unsolved * 100,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", percent(pct_unsolved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(incidents_unsolved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved),
    color = jr_pal[1],
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Violent crime unsolved rate in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%"))
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_us |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    pct_unsolved = pct_unsolved * 100,
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_unsolved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", percent(pct_unsolved / 100, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(incidents_unsolved, 1)
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_unsolved)

to_plot |>   
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime unsolved rate in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI <em>Crime in the U.S.</em>") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%"))
```

:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources

#### Crime and clearance rates

Data regarding crime and clearance rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2010 and 2020 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) in which law enforcement agencies submitted monthly counts of the four violent Part 1 Index Crimes (murder, aggravated assault, rape, and robbery). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime and clearances based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2020 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"}

National crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}

:::
